name: Agentic Radar
what_it_does: A security scanner that performs static analysis and runtime testing on agentic AI workflows, identifying vulnerabilities like prompt injection, PII leakage, and generating visual dependency graphs mapped to OWASP frameworks.
use_cases: Scanning agentic AI codebases for security vulnerabilities before deployment
Generating visual dependency graphs of agent workflows to understand tool and service exposure
Running adversarial prompt injection tests against OpenAI Agents-based systems
not_for: Providing MCP tools to agents - this scans agents, not empowers them
Frameworks not yet supported (only OpenAI Agents, CrewAI, LangGraph, n8n, Autogen)
best_when: You need to audit the security posture of an agentic AI system before production deployment.
avoid_when: You need an MCP server that provides tools to agents rather than scanning them for vulnerabilities.

auth.notes: Requires OPENAI_API_KEY or AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY for prompt hardening and runtime testing features. Static analysis scanning works without API keys.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed open source. Runtime testing features consume OpenAI API credits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool with HTML report output. Error handling details not prominent in docs but YAML config and framework-specific extras suggest reasonable error paths.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Static analysis is inherently idempotent - same input produces same output.
known_agent_gotchas: This is NOT an MCP server - it's a CLI security scanner. It detects MCP servers in scanned code but doesn't provide MCP tools.
Runtime testing only supports OpenAI Agents framework currently
Prompt hardening and testing features require OpenAI API keys and consume credits
